skill.name: maxhub-temp-mail
skill.description: Temp Mail Assistant for generating temporary email addresses, listing received emails, and retrieving email details via the MaxHub API.
skill.summary: A skill that creates disposable temporary email addresses and retrieves incoming messages through the MaxHub API.
skill.author: XieWxx

security_audit.summary: Security audit confirmed the skill is a legitimate MaxHub temp mail API client. Environment variable access (MAXHUB_API_KEY) and external command execution (curl) are inherent to the skill's documented functionality. All 21 hardcoded URL findings are false positives pointing to the skill's own API provider at www.aconfig.cn. The heuristic 'dangerous combination' finding is dismissed as expected API client behavior, not obfuscation. Weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives - Bearer token auth is standard REST API practice. System reconnaissance findings are false positives - endpoint listing is API documentation. The skill is safe to publish with appropriate warnings about credential handling and network access.

high_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Authentication
high_findings.description: The skill reads MAXHUB_API_KEY from environment variables. This is a user-configured credential used for Bearer token authentication to the MaxHub API. The access is intentional and documented, but the credential is sent over the network in HTTP Authorization headers, which is standard practice for REST API clients.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution via curl
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the AI to execute curl shell commands to interact with the MaxHub temp mail API. All curl commands target documented API endpoints at www.aconfig.cn and use a user-provided API key. No command injection vectors are present as curl arguments reference fixed API paths and environment variables.

dangerous_patterns.title: API Credential Sent in HTTP Authorization Header via curl
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines environment variable access (MAXHUB_API_KEY), network requests (to www.aconfig.cn), and external command execution (curl) to authenticate and query the temp mail API. The credential is interpolated into a Bash variable and passed as a Bearer token. While standard for API clients, this pattern means the API key is exposed in the process environment and transmitted over the network.

content.user_title: Generate disposable emails and receive messages instantly
content.value_statement: Users need temporary email addresses to protect their privacy during signups or testing. This skill solves that by generating disposable inboxes and retrieving incoming messages through the MaxHub API without exposing your real email address.
actual_capabilities: Generate a new temporary email address on demand
List all emails received in a temporary mailbox
Read the full content of a specific email by ID
Work with both GET and POST API methods
Handle errors with clear messages for auth failures, rate limits, and server errors
limitations: Requires a valid MAXHUB_API_KEY configured in the environment
Cannot send emails, only receive them
Email retention depends on the MaxHub service and may be time-limited
API rate limits may apply based on your MaxHub plan

use_cases.title: Privacy-conscious user registering on websites
use_cases.description: A user needs to sign up for a service but does not want to expose their personal email. They generate a temporary email, use it for registration, and retrieve the verification email through this skill.

use_cases.title: QA engineer automating email verification tests
use_cases.description: A tester needs to automate signup flows that require email verification. They generate temporary inboxes programmatically, poll for incoming messages, and extract verification codes for automated validation.

use_cases.title: Developer testing email-related features
use_cases.description: A developer building an application with email notifications needs to verify delivery. They use temporary inboxes to receive and inspect test emails without creating multiple real accounts.

prompt_templates.title: Create a temporary email address
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new temporary email address for me to use.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user who wants a disposable inbox

prompt_templates.title: Check for new messages
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me all emails in my temporary inbox.
prompt_templates.scenario: User checking for incoming messages in their temp mailbox

prompt_templates.title: Read a specific email
prompt_templates.prompt: Open the email with subject 'Verify your account' and show me the verification code.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to extract a code from a specific message

prompt_templates.title: Full registration workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a temp email, use it to register on example.com, then wait for the verification email and read the confirmation link inside.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user automating a complete signup flow

output_examples.input: Create a new temporary email address.
output_examples.output:
Your temporary email address is: temp-abc123@aconfig.cn

This inbox will receive messages for the next 24 hours. You can check for new emails anytime by asking me to list your messages.

output_examples.input: Check my inbox for new emails.
output_examples.output:
You have 2 new messages in your temporary inbox:

1. **Verify your account** - from no-reply@example.com - Received 2 minutes ago
2. **Welcome to our service** - from welcome@example.com - Received 1 minute ago

Reply with the email number to read its full content.

best_practices: Keep your MAXHUB_API_KEY secure and never share it in conversation output
Use a fresh temporary email for each service registration to maintain privacy
Check for emails shortly after triggering a send to avoid missing time-limited messages
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode API keys in prompts or skill configuration files
Do not use the same temporary email for multiple unrelated services to avoid inbox correlation
Do not expose the raw API response data without formatting it for readability

faq.question: How long does a temporary email address last?
faq.answer: The email address is valid for the duration of your session. Check the MaxHub service documentation for exact retention policies.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a MAXHUB_API_KEY. Sign up at www.aconfig.cn to get one. Set it as an environment variable or through your tool's configuration.

faq.question: Can I send emails from the temporary address?
faq.answer: No, this skill only supports receiving emails. It generates inboxes and reads incoming messages.

faq.question: What happens if my API key is invalid?
faq.answer: The skill will return a 401 Unauthorized error. Verify your MAXHUB_API_KEY is correctly configured and has not expired.

faq.question: Can I use this for automated testing?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports API-based workflows. You can generate inboxes, poll for messages, and extract content programmatically.

faq.question: Which languages does the skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports both English and Chinese. Responses are shown in the language matching your query.
